label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out the best title for the passage about the London Olympics. Let me read through the passage again to understand the main points. The passage starts by saying the London Olympics could be considered the "greenest" Games ever. It mentions using the bid concept of "a One Planet Olympics" to avoid environmental damage. They cleaned up polluted areas by removing 2 million tons of contaminated soil and tearing down 200 old factories. 99% of the debris was reused, like the steel tubes from old gas pipelines for the stadium roof. Venues were built with green techniques, such as the lightest stadium requiring less steel and concrete, and lighting pipes reducing electricity use. They dealt with waste by using rainwater and recycled wastewater, saving 30-40% water. Public transport was encouraged to reduce carbon emissions. Also, the anti-doping results were praised with only one athlete testing positive. The question is asking for the best title. The options are A) How to deal with the anti-doping, B) How to use recycled materials, C) London's green games, D) London's public transport. The main theme here is the overall green efforts of London during the Olympics, which includes recycling, green building, water conservation, public transport, and even anti-doping as part of their success. The first paragraph mentions "greenest" Games, so the title should reflect that comprehensive approach. Option C, "London's green games," seems to cover all these aspects. Option B is too narrow, focusing only on recycled materials. Option D is just one part of
